subject: Anthropology
title: State Violence
description: A quick, practical guide to State Violence.

hook.text: The state alone can legally kill, imprison, and wage war. Weber called this the monopoly on legitimate violence.

buildup.text: States claim the exclusive right to use force through police, military, and legal punishment systems.

discovery.text: This monopoly is what makes states different from other political forms. It's the defining feature of state power.

twist.text: When states abuse this monopoly, violence becomes oppression. The line between order and tyranny is always political.

climax.text: State violence is the foundation of order and the source of the greatest human suffering. It's power at its rawest.

punchline.text: The state's power rests on controlled violence.

quiz.question: What is the state's monopoly on violence?
options: Only the state can legally use force through police and military
